attacking_style: Launches long-range solo attacks from breakaways at 2km to go, exploiting technical course features (roundabouts, corners) that favor power over pure sprint speed. Timing often premature when in peloton scenarios.
team_tactics: Team (Dimension Data) provides front positioning for sprints, but rider struggles to capitalize, often wasting lead-out work with ill-timed accelerations above final kilometer.
terrain_preference: Technical finales with roundabouts/corners requiring sustained power rather than explosive acceleration. Flat to rolling parcours; struggles on any meaningful climbing.
vulnerability: Declining climbing ability evident when dropped from breakaways before final climbs. Prone to tactical errors with premature accelerations that waste energy and team positioning.
key_patterns: Attacks solo from breakaways at ~2km, using power-endurance hybrid advantage (2 instances)
Premature accelerations in bunch sprints compromise positioning and waste energy (2 instances)
Drops from groups on climbs despite breakaway presence, suggesting weak climbing (2 instances)
Exploits technical course features requiring sustained power over pure speed (2 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: 2019 observations show declining climbing capacity and repeated tactical errors compared to stronger 2017 performances
